_source: iNAV docs + src/main/fc/box.c. Fixed-wing relevance noted. Keys verified against live MSP_BOXNAMES on iNAV 6.1.0 (SPEEDYBEEF405WING) 2026-06-25 — box names can drift between major versions (e.g. 'AUTO TUNE'→'AUTOTUNE', 'AUTO LEVEL'→'AUTO LEVEL TRIM' on 9.x), so this glossary is keyed to 6.x names.

ARM.description: Arms the flight controller, enabling motor output.
ARM.notes: Must be assigned to a dedicated switch. FC will not arm without this.

ANGLE.description: Self-levelling mode — FC corrects roll/pitch to return to level. Accelerometer required.
ANGLE.notes: Most beginner-friendly mode. Good for FPV wings. Requires ACC calibration.

HORIZON.description: Self-levelling near centre, allows full stick deflection for aerobatics.
HORIZON.notes: Mix of ANGLE and MANUAL. Returns to level when sticks are centered.

MANUAL.description: Full manual / passthrough — no stabilisation. Pilot controls servos directly.
MANUAL.notes: Needed for maiden flights to check servo directions without gyro interference.

NAV ALTHOLD.description: Altitude hold — FC maintains current altitude using baro/GPS.
NAV ALTHOLD.notes: Requires baro and/or GPS. Throttle stick controls target altitude.

NAV POSHOLD.description: Position hold — FC maintains current GPS position.
NAV POSHOLD.notes: Fixed-wing position hold is a loiter. Requires GPS 3D fix.

NAV RTH.description: Return to Home — FC navigates back to launch point and lands/loiters.
NAV RTH.notes: Requires GPS. Set 'nav_rth_altitude' appropriately for your flying area.

NAV WP.description: Waypoint navigation — flies a pre-loaded mission.
NAV WP.notes: Requires GPS and a mission uploaded via Configurator or GCS.

FAILSAFE.description: Forces failsafe action immediately when the switch is active.
FAILSAFE.notes: Useful for testing failsafe behaviour. Do not leave accidentally assigned.

HEADING HOLD.description: Maintains the current magnetic heading (yaw hold).
HEADING HOLD.notes: Requires compass. Useful for long-range FPV.

TURN ASSIST.description: Coordinated-turn assistance — automates rudder during banked turns.
TURN ASSIST.notes: Makes flying a conventional fixed wing easier. Set 'fw_turn_assist_yaw_gain'.

SERVO AUTOTRIM.description: Automatically trims servos during straight-and-level flight.
SERVO AUTOTRIM.notes: Activate for a few seconds in level flight to auto-trim. Saves to config.

OSD OFF.description: Switches the OSD overlay off (hides all OSD elements).
OSD OFF.notes: Board box name is 'OSD OFF' on iNAV 6.x (was 'OSD SW' on older versions). Useful to hide the OSD for clean DVR recording.

BEEPER.description: Activates the FC buzzer to help locate a lost model.
BEEPER.notes: Assign to a switch for lost-model finder functionality.

KILLSWITCH.description: Immediately disarms and kills motor output.
KILLSWITCH.notes: Emergency stop. Use with care — activating in flight will crash the aircraft.

LOITER CHANGE.description: Change loiter direction in fixed-wing loiter/poshold.

NAV CRUISE.description: Cruise control — maintains speed and heading without pilot input.
NAV CRUISE.notes: Requires GPS and pitot tube for accurate airspeed.

AUTO TUNE.notes: Board box name is 'AUTO TUNE' on iNAV 6.x (renamed 'AUTOTUNE' on 9.x). Fly in a safe area with plenty of altitude. Requires stable conditions.

NAV LAUNCH.description: Hand-launch detection — waits for throw, then applies motor/elevator.
NAV LAUNCH.notes: Great for flying wings without landing gear. Configure 'nav_fw_launch_*' settings. Note: on a fixed-wing target this is usually auto-triggered on arm and may not appear as an assignable box unless enabled — it is not in the active box list on the 6.1.0 SP4W.

CAMSTAB.description: Camera stabilisation — uses gimbal outputs to level a camera mount.

AUTO LEVEL.description: Auto Level Trim — in-flight auto-tuning of the pitch trim offset a fixed-wing needs to fly straight and level.
AUTO LEVEL.notes: Board box name is 'AUTO LEVEL' on iNAV 6.x (renamed 'AUTO LEVEL TRIM' on 9.x). Hold level flight briefly with this active to capture the pitch offset.

SOARING.description: Soaring mode — cuts the motor and holds altitude/attitude for thermalling in sailplanes and motor gliders.
SOARING.notes: Glider-oriented. Requires baro; pairs well with ALTHOLD / cruise nav modes.

NAV COURSE HOLD.description: Course hold — holds a GPS ground course (heading-over-ground) while you keep pitch/throttle control.
NAV COURSE HOLD.notes: Requires GPS. Great for relaxed FPV cruising; the fixed-wing equivalent of a heading lock.

FLAPERON.description: Flaperon — droops both ailerons by a preset offset to act as flaps for slower, steeper approaches.
FLAPERON.notes: Set the throw via 'flaperon_throw_offset'. Useful for landing on conventional wings; not typically needed on flying wings.

PREARM.description: Prearm — a safety gate that must be active before ARM will engage, preventing accidental arming.
PREARM.notes: Assign to a momentary or second switch. ARM is refused (with a prearm error) until PREARM is active.
